flashcards.q: What name is given to naturally occurring magnets discovered in ancient times?
Name three common metals used to make artificial magnets.
Common magnet shapes: bar, horseshoe, cylindrical, and ____.
Terms: Materials attracted toward a magnet.
Terms: Materials like plastic, wood, and glass that are not attracted by magnets.
What are the two ends of a magnet called?
In a bar magnet, relative magnetic strength is maximum where?
True or False: A magnet can be broken into pieces to isolate a single pole.
flashcards.a: False; poles always exist in pairs.

flashcards.q: What is the constant resting direction of a freely suspended magnet?
Property: Why does a freely suspended magnet align itself North-South?
flashcards.a: Earth behaves like a giant magnet.

flashcards.q: Terminology: The end of the magnet pointing toward the geographic North.
Device: Uses a small magnetic needle to find directions.
Historical tool: The ancient Indian magnetized fish device for sea navigation.
Which pole of a compass needle is typically painted red?
How can you make a temporary magnet out of an iron needle?
flashcards.a: By using the stroking method with a permanent magnet.

flashcards.q: Rule: Like poles (N-N, S-S) of two magnets ____ each other.
Rule: Unlike poles (N-S) of two magnets ____ each other.
What is the sure test to confirm if a piece of metal is a magnet?
Can magnetic effects act through non-magnetic materials like wood and glass?
Three actions that cause demagnetization: heating, hammering, and ____.
Tool name: Pieces of soft iron used across the ends of stored magnets.
Storage: Bar magnets should be kept in pairs with ____ poles on the same side.
Precaution: Keep magnets away from which common electronic device?
What is the small circular box with a magnetic needle inside called?
Observation: Which part of a bar magnet picks up the fewest iron filings?
Discovery: Where was the lodestone first found according to tradition?
Terms: Materials that are not attracted to a magnet.
What happens when you bring the North pole of a magnet near the South pole of a compass?
What happens when you bring the South pole of a magnet near the South pole of a compass?
What was the purpose of the dial in an ancient magnetic compass?
flashcards.a: To indicate all major directions (N, S, E, W).
